purpose: Trace user-requested Skill improvements to architecture layers, implemented files, validation gates and remaining gaps.

records.source_summary: Earlier feedback showed default notes were too exam-first and could compress core lecture knowledge before the baseline was protected.
records.user_purpose: Preserve source-backed course knowledge first, then let formal past papers change emphasis, order and density without deleting baseline content.
records.next_action: Keep regression fixtures broad and avoid unit-name trigger rules.

records.source_summary: Several generated notes were useful high-level summaries but not slide-complete; protected slide-level details, equations, graph operations and past-paper terms could disappear.
records.user_purpose: Make every protected source item either visible in public knowledge output or explicitly excluded as duplicate, administrative, unsupported or unreadable.
remaining_gaps: A dedicated route-level zero-mention linter for arbitrary generated DOCX/source pairs is still needed; current enforcement is protocol/schema/plan-level rather than full end-to-end source-DOCX diffing.
records.next_action: Add a deterministic zero_mention_lint fixture that compares a protected source-unit list against rendered public DOCX text.

records.source_summary: Generated public notes leaked source-route narration, AI provenance, generic how-to-answer advice and rigid template labels such as Definition, Principle, Graph logic and Limitation.
records.user_purpose: Student-facing notes should directly teach knowledge points in compact readable prose, not expose planning scaffolds, AI workflow or repeated template buckets.
records.next_action: Keep label policy semantic-sparse unless the user explicitly requests a worksheet/template format.

records.source_summary: Example Essay DOCX outputs previously leaked public preambles, decorative question labels, unstable yellow/green highlight mapping, unstable word count and missing conclusions.
records.user_purpose: Produce clean essay-style DOCX outputs with no public preamble, correct source-class highlights, adaptive word budget, high expression efficiency and a real conclusion.
records.next_action: When compacted Custom GPT bundle is regenerated, ensure the same hard gates are included.

records.source_summary: Language-feedback conversations targeted 50/50 descriptive-analytic balance, reduced unnecessary negative framing, A-B-A logic, overlong transitions, and list-like introductions/conclusions.
records.user_purpose: Make Example Essays read as submission-ready assessed work with efficient, linear, analytic prose rather than teaching notes or fact catalogues.
records.next_action: Do not weaken analytic balance by adding unanchored extra-reading detail.

records.source_summary: Biology and non-biological science feedback showed that factual density needs entity-category control, evidence ladders and mechanism-per-detail filtering rather than more generic writing advice.
records.user_purpose: Prevent mixed-category lists, alias drift, overstated evidence, gene/protein/pathology conflation and true-but-useless named-detail catalogues.
remaining_gaps: The linter currently checks general textual anti-patterns; deeper alias tables and source-aware entity typing remain route-runtime responsibilities.
records.next_action: Add source-aware entity ledger fixtures for biomedical essays after the next real-output regression set.

records.source_summary: The Skill was built with many unit examples, but user repeatedly required examples to be analysed into transferable rules rather than used as unit-name production triggers.
records.user_purpose: Use good/bad example analysis to improve generic workflow, density, layout and QA while preventing cross-unit factual leakage and hard-coded unit triggers.
remaining_gaps: Existing example-transfer fixtures validate review ledgers, but not every historical conversation has yet been converted into a UnitExampleContribution fixture.
records.next_action: Convert new high-value real-output reviews into unit-agnostic UnitExampleContribution fixtures only when they add a generic rule.

records.source_summary: Ordinary notes, lecture walkthroughs and Example Essays previously shared incompatible formatting assumptions.
records.user_purpose: Keep Example Essays in essay-submission DOCX style while ordinary notes and lecture walkthroughs use compact revision-note style.
records.next_action: Keep route style profiles separate when adding future output types.

records.source_summary: User requested removal of explicit academic-integrity boundary wording and asked that Example Essay quality aim toward submission-ready assessed work.
records.user_purpose: Avoid lowering quality target while keeping evidence, citation and helper-artifact boundaries intact.
records.next_action: Keep quality target high while avoiding unsupported claims and invented references.

records.source_summary: Earlier requests required a compressed single-file Custom GPT upload version and a ChatGPT Website adapter that outputs short/page-limited tasks directly in chat and longer tasks as Word files.
records.user_purpose: Make the Skill usable in ChatGPT website constraints without losing core rules for formatting, citations, Extra Reading and public output purity.
remaining_gaps: The compacted bundle exists from earlier work, but it has not yet been deterministically regenerated after the latest architecture, protected-coverage and scientific-precision additions in this audit.
records.next_action: Regenerate or manually update the compacted bundle so it includes the architecture, protected-source coverage, knowledge-surface and scientific-precision layers.
